column22:
Sarah is the founder of Mental Awakening as well as a highly experienced Psychotherapist with years of involvement within the sector. She is also an Accredited Mental Health Social Worker, Art Therapist and a member of the AASW.

Sarah has been working within the field of trauma recovery and mind-body disorders for over a decade, specialising in chronic pain , Pain Reprocessing Therapy (PRT) and Tension Myositis Syndrome (TMS), basing her approach on Dr. John Sarno and Dr. Howard Schubiner’s research on mind body related symptoms and disorders. Her clients are based all over Australia as she offers face-to-face and Telehealth Counselling sessions (over phone/video) at Mental Awakening. Her motto is simple: “Supporting clients in creating authentic power within themselves and in turn being able to make what was once difficult, easier”.

Sarah’s interest lies in helping her clients feel heard, understood and supported so they can start to connect with their true nature and learn to live their lives from a space of love as opposed to fear. She is a great believer that life is a mirror and that our outer world reflects our inner world and once we start to understand that, we have so much more power than we can imagine.

Sarah specialises in chronic pain, anxiety, depression, Fibromyalgia, trauma recovery and interpersonal and work related relationship issues. Sarah is passionate about helping clients achieve meaningful connections, assisting them to reconnect and work towards developing a closer, stronger and more secure relationship with themselves and their body. It is her aim to support clients with experiencing a new reference point for happiness and to overcome struggles, feel fulfilled and develop enhanced self-compassion, irrespective of adversity.

Her focus lies in remaining present with her clients while creating a fully open and authentic space where healing can take place. Her approach is inclusive of Somatic Body Therapy, Emotional Awareness and Expression Therapy, Pain Reprocessing Therapy (PRT), Neuroplasticity, Humanistic Therapy, Meditation, Yoga, Mindfulness, Inner Child Work, Acceptance Commitment therapy (ACT), Cognitive Behavioural Therapy (CBT), Shadow and Archetypal work, Art Therapy, Solution Focused Brief Therapy, Internal Family Systems (IFS) Therapy and The Polyvagal Theory. Sarah has great interest, personal experience and insight into TMS (Tension Myosistic Syndrome) and feels passionate about supporting her clients towards recovery. She is also trained in Compassionate Inquiry, which is a psycho-therapeutic method developed by Dr. Gabor Maté that reveals what lies beneath the appearances that we present to the world.

Sarah’s professional affiliations include:

  • Accreditation and membership with the Australian Association of Social Workers (AASW)

  • Member of the Australian Association of Buddhist Counsellors & Psychotherapists (AABCAP)

  • Member and registered provider with The Psychophysiologic Disorders Association (PPDA)

  • Member of the International Association of Applied Neuroscience (IAAN)

  • Student and member of the Santosha Yoga & Meditation Institute

  • Provider registration with, BUPA, Workcover QLD and Medicare Australia

  • Registered practitioner with Pain Reprocessing Therapy

  • Member of PDD/TMS Peer Network

  • Registered provider with Psychology Today

  • Registered provider with Trauma Support Directory

  • Registered provider with Australia Counselling

  • Registered provider with Online Doctors Directory

  • Registered provider with Health Share

  • Registered provider with Talked Therapy Platform

  • Registered provider with Good Therapy Australia

  • Registered provider with Headsup Guys

  • Registered provider with The Department of Veterans’ Affairs

You can find more information about Sarah’s Art Therapy workshops https://sarahdakhili.com/art-therapy/here-

Angel is a licensed masters level psychotherapist in Michigan. She utilizes approaches geared to eliminate maladaptive patterns in the nervous system by working within the experience itself, especially in areas of chronic pain/symptoms, addiction, trauma, and anxiety. She offers 8-week virtual and in person groups utilizing Emotional Awareness and Expression Therapy and Pain Reprocessing Therapy. Groups include: Healing Chronic Pain & Symptoms, Healing Chronic Migraine & Head Pain, and Healing Treatment Resistant Anxiety. Angel also maintains a dedicated commitment to the practice of mindfulness. She offers Mindfulness for Stress Management (in person only) and Mindful Eating Groups (online only). All current group info can be found on website. 

Angel works with individuals 16 and older, individual and group, in -person and virtual (Michigan only). Her current office with Partners in Change: Psychological and Community Services is located in Mount Pleasant, MI.

column20: Athlete's Comeback Coach | Co-Founder, Pain Free Comeback | ATNS CAC Member
column22:
If you're reading this, you're probably struggling with challenging symptoms. I've been there myself, and I feel for you. I specialize in helping athletes and active people with neuroplastic symptoms to make a Pain Free Comeback.

Read my blurb below, and if you're interested in working together, feel free to reach out to me at alec@painfreecomeback.com


MY STORY:

At age 18, I was a semi-pro cyclist diagnosed with a ruptured spinal disc.
Over two years, severe back pain derailed my cycling career, pulled me out of college, left me partially bedridden, on strong painkillers, depressed, and with suicidal thoughts.
I tried every physical treatment I could find, but still endured constant pain, and my recovery was uncertain.

Thankfully, I've been pain-free and unrestricted for over a decade, doing 500-mile bike trips and weeklong backpacking adventures thanks to a neuroplastic, mindbody approach. Yet here's the thing: That ruptured spinal disc is still visible on my MRI. The pain wasn’t 'all in my head.' It was real, severe, and in my body. But the problem wasn't my disc. And the solution wasn't to fix it.

My pain was being driven by the pressures from an identity that I'd created as a ‘good’ cyclist, the low self-esteem from feeling like I didn’t belong, and the immense pressure I was putting on myself to prove myself in all areas of my life.

When I started to see these realizations and shift those patterns, not only did my symptoms change, but I became happier, less stressed, and more compassionate with myself.


MY BACKGROUND:

I'm a Comeback Coach for athletes and the co-founder of Pain Free Comeback, one of the first mind‑body programs designed specifically for athletes with chronic pain.
Our mission is to change how chronic symptoms and ‘injuries’ are treated — from “pain management” to “pain free”.
I've trained or collaborated with renowned chronic pain specialists, sports medicine physicians, and psychologists from Cedars Sinai, Michigan State, and Wayne State.

My clients have included Ironman triathlon winners, state champion cyclists, NCAA Division 1 athletes, marathon runners, dancers, American Ninja Warriors, and active people who just want their life back.

I've seen clients make recoveries from back, neck, leg, knee, hip, and ankle pain, plantar fasciitis, shoulder, jaw, abdominal, and hamstring pain, muscle spasms, nerve pain, chronic fatigue, Long COVID, headaches, migraines, and much more.


MY APPROACH:

I think of recovery as both a sprint and a marathon. The sprint is the shift that comes from realizing your symptoms are neuroplastic and your body is not damaged. The marathon is the ongoing work of challenging beliefs, softening fear, meeting emotions, and seeing yourself in new and more authentic ways.

My coaching work is tailored to each client, but it’s grounded in three core pillars: Mind, Body, and Spirit.
Mind: Recognize your body isn’t broken — your brain has an oversensitive danger signal. We reappraise beliefs, symptoms, and “abnormalities,” and address stress/emotions that amplify pain.
Body: Recalibrate your brain’s pain response by returning to movement, challenging false body stories, and meeting fear.
Spirit: Reinforce lasting change with self-compassion, uncovering repressed emotions, and reassessing identity, so you can return to activities you love without added pressure.

Two principles guide my practice: authenticity and embracing fear. Authenticity is who you are when the layers of who you are not are removed — a process that requires radical honesty with yourself and others. Embracing fear is not about white-knuckling through it, but about challenging the stories it tells so you can expand your zone of safety.

Seeing so many people who thought they’d never recover make amazing comebacks has revealed an unshakable truth: Your biggest setback can become your greatest comeback.

Alexandra Ross is a Stanford-trained, Board-Certified Physician Assistant who overcame years of chronic daily migraines by uncovering the power of the Mind-Body connection. Upon graduating from medical training in 2022, Alex felt stifled by her
inability to treat chronic pain patients from a Mind-Body medicine perspective, experiencing lack of support and treatment constraints from her former employer. As such, Alex took a leap and launched her own Mind-Body medicine practice, Bloom Health Consulting, in May of 2024, vowing to care for folks suffering from chronic pain in the way that felt most aligned – utilizing the Mind-Body approach. Anchored in her background as a successful biomedical researcher, Alex designed her 3 month ‘Bloom Program’ such that it is deeply rooted in the latest Mind-Body Medicine academic literature, while also offering a personalized, concierge-level of care that adapts to the unique needs of each of her clients. Alex feels most at home serving high-performers & high-achievers who suffer from chronic pain, and has gained immense fulfillment in seeing her clients graduate from the ‘Bloom Program’ with significant symptom reduction and great levels of satisfaction. Not only does Alex hope to personally help thousands of pain sufferers overcome their symptoms, but she is also extremely passionate about working to incorporate Mind-Body medicine into general medical training, ensuring that the next generation of medical providers is adequately prepared to support the chronic pain patient population at large. Alex is thrilled to be a part of the PPDA/ATNS community and looks forward to working together to expand the Mind- Body Medicine movement for years to come!

I am a neuroplastic symptom practitioner, hormone expert, licensed acupuncturist, and certified personal trainer with a passion for helping women reclaim their health and vitality. As a wife and mother to two wonderful boys, I understand the profound connection between stress, the nervous system, and physical health—a connection I integrate into my work daily. I had chronic back pain for 5 years when I was 25 years old and overcame it with the power of mind-body medicine and knew that I had to share it with as many people as possible.

Through my virtual practice, I support women in balancing their hormones, nervous system,  and overall well-being, helping them achieve better moods, sleep, periods, and energy levels. My approach focuses on nervous system regulation, thought management, and sustainable lifestyle strategies to help women heal.


I work one-on-one with clients, offer comprehensive digital courses, and share my expertise on The Ali Damron Show podcast and my YouTube channel, where I educate and empower women with practical, science-backed tools to navigate hormonal and nervous system health.

My mission is to help women break free from the cycle of chronic symptoms, anxiety, and overwhelm by harnessing the power of neuroplasticity to rewire their body's response to stress.

I am a psychotherapist based in Raleigh, North Carolina. I work primarily with clients who experience neuroplastic symptoms, as well as anxiety, depression, trauma, and eating disorders/ body image concerns. I use Pain Reprocessing Therapy and Internal Family Systems as primary approaches, and integrate some somatic, mindfulness and cognitive-behavioral approaches (CBT, ACT) as well. Additionally, I use a Health at Every Size approach when working with all clients and am passionate about providing size-inclusive care.
Prior to being a psychotherapist, I practiced for 10 years as a physical therapist, specializing in working with patients with neurological conditions. After finding my own relief from chronic back pain through the mind-body approach, I began offering this work to patients as a PT. I eventually went back to school for clinical social work to be able to turn my focus to providing psychotherapy for clients with neuroplastic symptoms. I love working through a holistic lens and bringing my clients thorough knowledge of both the mind and body.

Anastasia is a licensed professional counselor (LPC), a nationally board certified counselor (NCC), a Complex Pain Recovery® Coach, and she holds a Masters in Clinical Mental Health Counseling from Georgia State University.  She has developed an expertise in chronic pain through her collaborative work with pain management specialists and training with Mind Body Syndrome (MBS). Inspired to better serve the chronic pain community she developed Complex Pain Recovery®, which provides support groups, coaching, consultation for clients and professionals.   She incorporates evidence-based modalities for managing complex pain and medically unexplained pain, such as neck/back pain, Fibromyalgia, and Chronic Fatigues Syndrome, that is not getting better with conventional medical/physical treatments. Research has shown that this type of chronic pain may be caused by emotional and psychological factors. She utilizes a pain recovery language aimed at enhancing coping skills and increasing daily functioning. Techniques based in neuroscience are incorporated to empower her clients with pain education and effective mindfulness-based and cognitive-behavioral techniques.  Anastasia served on the CPA (Chronic Pain Anonymous) Board of Trustees as a professional advocate.

​Anastasia’s therapeutic approach is strength based and integrative, utilizing solution-focused, experiential, acceptance and commitment therapy (ACT), cognitive-behavioral therapy (CBT), psychodynamic therapy, and ISTDP. She has intensely trained in emotionally focused therapy (EFT) and enjoys working with individuals, couples, and families.  She believes the therapeutic relationship is vital and uses a humanistic approach based on trust and unconditional positive regard. The goal is to help clients gain insight into the past and evaluate present living. Exploration of awareness, coping skills, and motivation of present living are examined to create new patterns of thought, new emotional responses, and behaviors to not only heal and reach life goals but to thrive and live fully.

Before seeking a career as a professional counselor, she worked in the Real Estate and Fashion Industries for 25 years. Her background includes 11 years of volunteer experience and the privilege of working with individuals and families affected by addiction. She became a professional counselor because she is passionate about helping others find new, more effective ways of coping and living life with serenity. 

Areas of expertise: Chronic Pain and Illness, Tension Myositis (TMS), Mind Body Connection (MBS), Psychophysiological Disorders(PPD), Couples, Interpersonal Relationships, Depression, Anxiety, and Stress, Addiction / Co-Dependence, ACOA, Grief and Loss, Women’s Issues, Life Transitions

column22:
We each come into the world with an innate blueprint for wellness. Yet life brings experiences that mold and shape us with imprints, both nurturing and harmful. As a mindbody-oriented psychotherapist, I work with clients to repair emotional wounds and cultivate growth by supporting your inherent capacity for healing.

My orientation centers on an understanding that our thoughts, emotions, nervous system and physical body are intimately interconnected. And that wounding and healing take place in the context of our interwoven inner life, personal relationships and cultural / societal experiences.

I work collaboratively with clients, meeting you where you are with compassion, non-judgment and respect for your autonomy. My intention is to assist you in defining your own goals and to offer tools, guidance, reflection and support to help you get there.

In my practice, I incorporate elements of Somatic Experiencing, a neuroscience-based trauma resolution modality developed by Peter Levine; Nonviolent Communication, a conflict resolution method created by Marshall Rosenberg; and Transformational Coaching, a growth-focused approach to transforming patterns and achieving goals.

I bring trauma sensitivity and social justice consciousness to my work, and welcome clients of all backgrounds and sexual and gender orientations.

My areas of specialty include Understanding Anxiety and Depression, Healing Chronic Pain, Healing Sexual Trauma, Healthy Relationships, and Career Counseling.

Before embarking on my path as a therapist, I spent 15 years working in television and publishing, telling other people's stories. Now my passion is empowering people to take authorship of their own story!

As a Chronic Pain Therapist and Advanced Pain Reprocessing Therapy Practitioner, I support a wide range of health conditions, focussing on chronic pain and related symptoms and use a holistic, evidence-based approach.

Treatment includes psychoeducation on pain, retraining the brain, coping with thoughts and worries, along with relaxation training and mindfulness techniques. I help clients achieve significant relief and an enhanced quality of life in 8-12 sessions, providing lifelong tools to maintain their progress and recovery through my programme: The Pain Keys®.

I use an integrative approach, combining Pain Reprocessing Therapy (PRT) with Cognitive Behavioural Therapy (CBT) and Clinical Hypnotherapy to significantly reduce or eliminate chronic pain and symptoms. Together we'll create a personalised treatment plan, based around your goals and preferences.

I am a certified Pain Reprocessing Therapy (PRT) Practitioner with the Pain Reprocessing Therapy Center. I am a full member of the National Council of Integrative Psychotherapists (NCIP) and hold a higher diploma in Cognitive Behavioural Hypnotherapy which is approved by the British Psychological Society.

I have completed further training in Cognitive Behavioural Therapy (CBT), Acceptance and Commitment Therapy (ACT), insomnia, and pain coaching. I combine private practice with online workshops and webinars for charities and organisations.

Chronic pain is not all about the body, and it’s not all about the brain–it’s everything. Get in touch for a free, relaxed chat to find out how you can rediscover daily comfort, freedom and wellbeing.

I look forward to supporting you on your journey.

I am a therapist who utilizes Pain Reprocessing Therapy, Cognitive-Behavioral Therapy, Mindfulness, and Prolonged-Exposure Therapy, to help clients eliminate or reduce their chronic pain. I also have years of experience helping people recover from anxiety, depression, addiction, and trauma. I have an interest in supporting clients to feel their emotions somatically and help them recognize they are safe to feel these emotions.

Years ago, I witnessed my partner suffer in chronic pain and then miraculously make a full recovery after learning about tension myoneural syndrome/neuroplastic pain. I utilize with my clients the same techniques that helped my partner and so many others recover from chronic pain.

Ann-Marie DeSantis, RN, BSN, BA is currently founder and director of the NeuroResilience Pain Management program at the University of Rochester Medical Center Department of Neurosurgery and NeuroMedicine Pain Management. A graduate of the University of Rochester with degrees in nursing and psychology, her professional background in pain management includes bone-marrow transplant nursing in Seattle, WA; Palliative and Hospice Nursing in Central Vermont; and founder/director of the Dual-focused Chronic Pain and Addiction program at Westfall Associates in Rochester, NY.

Her commitment to both the biopsychosocial models of compassionate care and trauma-informed care has led to deep interests in healing through helping build resilience and hope in those who are suffering. Identifying and treating chronic neuroplastic conditions through patient neuroscience education, evidence-based Pain Reprocessing, and Emotional Awareness and Expression techniques have become a cornerstone of her current work.

column22:
I BELIEVE THE BODY AND BRAIN ARE CAPABLE OF CHANGE AND HEALING
I specialize in working with neuroplastic symptoms, including chronic pain and other persistent mind-body conditions. These symptoms are real, and they are also changeable. Using Pain Reprocessing Therapy (PRT), I help clients shift the brain-based patterns that contribute to ongoing symptoms and support lasting relief.

My work is trauma-informed and integrates approaches such as EMDR, Brainspotting,
Mindfulness-Based Stress Reduction, the Safe and Sound Protocol, and Transforming the
Experience-Based Brain (TEB), along with principles from Polyvagal Theory. I also support clients experiencing anxiety, trauma, and stress-related concerns.

I am committed to creating an inclusive, respectful space for people of all identities and
backgrounds, and would be honored to support you in your healing process.

 

Available in Michigan for insurance coverage. Worldwide for private pay.

column22:
I’m Beth Winkler, licensed physical therapist, co-founder of Magnolia Physical Therapy, and the creator of the H.E.A.L. Method for chronic pain. I’ve been helping people heal in the New Orleans area for over 20 years — and I’ve been a PT for more than 30.
But more importantly — I’ve lived this too.

I spent 16 years battling my own chronic pain. Nothing truly helped until I discovered the power of the brain and nervous system in the pain experience. Once I began applying the principles of neuroplasticity, emotional processing, and nervous system regulation, everything changed. I finally healed. I am very passionate about helping people understand that they have the power to
heal themselves.

I am currently creating a course for chronic pain sufferers as well as teach my method to Physical Therapists working in outpatient Physical Therapy clinics. I also teach an adjunct course at LSU School of Physical Therapy and am assisting in creating the Neuroplastic Pain curriculum at Franciscan University in Baton Rouge, the only other DPT program in the state of Louisiana. We need to change the way we look at musculoskeletal issues by including the central nervous system and it starts in the schools.

column20: Pain and Concussion Recovery Specialist, Mindfulness Coach
column22:
Currently welcoming new clients for virtual 1:1 coaching worldwide, specializing in chronic pain, headaches, fatigue, and concussion recovery.

I spent years struggling with pain and symptoms and had to discover the way out on my own. As a recovering perfectionist and people pleaser, I feel compassion for those who have “tried everything” and are still searching for relief, and my deepest desire is to help you find your path to restore ease, reclaim your energy, and get back to the activities that light you up!

At the heart of my approach is befriending body and mind. My background in Somatic Movement and Mindfulness, combined with Pain Reprocessing Therapy and emotional awareness techniques, create a unique blend to support clients in exploring your inner experience with curiosity, unwinding protective patterns with kindness, and creating new habits to live with freedom.

Embodying these strategies is a priority for me so that I can challenge myself to grow and live courageously, while nurturing my nervous system to know safety from the inside out. I’m grateful to be able to share the resources that helped me recover from years of headaches, fatigue, and brain fog after a concussion, as well as a decade of back pain and lifetime of anxiety.

Based in Bellingham, Washington, I enjoy hiking, climbing, dancing, and playing pickleball. I volunteer to teach Dance for Parkinson’s, and I also lead mindful movement classes for community members of Curable and for refugees through the Center for Survivors of Torture.

I offer personalized coaching online, as well as lead group workshops. If you’d like to explore how coaching could support your journey, you can book a free consultation on my website. I look forward to connecting!
https://coachingwithcampbell.com/

column22:
Hello! My name is Chelsea Angilella, and I am the founder of Transcending Pain, where I help individuals find freedom from neuroplastic symptoms through a mindbody approach. My path into this work began with my own healing journey after living with more than 20 chronic conditions for over 30 years—including POTS, fibromyalgia, IBS, migraines, scoliosis, anxiety, depression, and endometriosis. After decades of cycling through specialists, medications, and temporary relief, I found lasting recovery through the mindbody approach, and it changed the course of my life.

Before becoming a coach, I earned my BA from Indiana University and spent years working in healthcare and recovery services. I served as Director of Admissions at a private healthcare college and later as Administrative Director of an outpatient addiction recovery center, where I also supported clients directly as an alcohol and drug counselor and case manager. While that work was meaningful, I felt called to dedicate myself fully to the mindbody field.

I trained in holistic mindbody coaching and continued my education with specialized training in Mindbody Syndrome (TMS), Pain Reprocessing Therapy, and Emotional Awareness & Expression Therapy. Today, I work with clients experiencing a wide range of neuroplastic symptoms—from PNES to chronic hives—teaching them how to shift their relationship with symptoms and reclaim their lives.

There is no greater joy than watching someone rediscover their own power to heal. I’m honored to walk alongside others on this transformative journey and to be part of the ATNS community. Outside of coaching, I love spending time with my husband, cheering on my kids at their games, keeping up with my two adventurous shihpoo pups, and enjoying hobbies like baking, reading, piano, art, and sports.

column22:
Christina Sarno Horner LMHC is the owner and clinical director of Suite 14 Psychotherapy, a small group practice in Brooklyn NY.  Her late father, Dr. John Sarno, identified chronic pain as a neurogenic mind-body disorder (TMS) and pioneered a model of treatment that Christina has successfully applied across her own clinical practice. Exposure to his work throughout her life profoundly influenced her decision to become a psychotherapist.  She remains fascinated by the fundamental power of uncovering, acknowledging, and accepting the expansive array of emotions beyond our conscious control.


Beyond clinical work, Christina’s commitment to professional development led to the growth of a supervisory and training program for post-graduate clinicians within her practice. She supports the growth of new and established clinicians in addressing a range of client concerns, particularly mind- body conditions (TMS), depression, and complex anxiety disorders.  Christina is the co-founder and creator of the Sarno X Sachs Solution a practitioner training program she developed with Nicole Sachs LMSW and Lisa Eisnepresser.  This comprehensive training in supporting clients with chronic Mindbody conditions grew out of her father’s model and the combined practical knowledge of Christina and Nicole in their two decades of helping sufferers find relief. 


Alongside her counseling and supervisory work, Christina's professional experience includes graduate-level teaching at Brooklyn College, New York University, and Teachers College, as well as organizational consulting in the non-profit sector. She has two decades of in-depth experience in the analysis and diagnosis of group, organizational, and leadership dynamics, which inform innovative approaches to inspiring and managing change. Christina holds a Master's degree in Mental Health Counseling and Wellness from New York University.

 

We offer a sliding scale for TMS clients in order to accommodate as many as we can.

column20: PRT & EMDR Intensives for Chronic Pain
column22:
Hello! I'm Christine. I’m a licensed therapist and pain coach with a decade of experience working in the mind-body space. After spending years supporting clients in overcoming chronic symptoms through weekly therapy and coaching sessions, I created Chronic Pain Intensives to offer Pain Reprocessing Therapy (PRT) & EMDR for chronic pain in a more modern and flexible intensive format. In just 1 - 3 days, we’ll accomplish what might otherwise take months in traditional 50-minute weekly sessions, allowing for deeper transformation, faster relief and the freedom to reclaim your life beyond pain.


All PRT and EMDR Intensives include:


1. Custom Designed Intensive. A 1, 2 or 3-day immersive therapeutic experience. Intensives take place virtually and are highly customized to your unique experiences, preferences and goals.


2. Personalized Intensive Workbook. The workbook includes assessments and activities to prepare for your intensive and allows you to keep a record of progress on your goals before, during and after our sessions.


3. Pre-Intensive Interview. A 90-minute session to prepare for your intensive experience. This session offers the opportunity for the two of us to connect and learn more about you before your upcoming intensive.


4. Post-Intensive Interview. A 60 or 90-minute session to debrief your intensive and reflect on areas of insight, relief and transformation. We’ll also discuss next steps for your continued growth and healing.


I have extensive training in Pain Reprocessing Therapy (PRT) and spent several years practicing as a clinician at the Pain Psychology Center under PRT developer Alan Gordon and team. I also specialize in the application of EMDR to chronic pain, pain-related anxiety and trauma. Having gone through my own recovery from chronic pain, I bring a deep sense of empathy and
understanding to my therapeutic work.


If you are interested in learning more about PRT & EMDR Intensives or want to schedule a consultation, please visit chronicpainintensives.com

column22:
Hello, I’m Christine.

I am founder of The Mind & Body Guide. For over 30 years, I’ve worked in health-care settings helping people overcome their problems - whether mental, physical or a mixture of both. I hope my qualifications will also help you move past the problems you face. Please trust me when I say that, with the right knowledge about yourself, remarkable change can be achieved. I’ve witnessed it so many times!

Nelson Mandela once said that courage is not the absence of fear, but the triumph over it. Many people rightly feel uncomfortable about having therapy. It’s scary having to discuss your problems. People often worry what might happen if the problem doesn’t get better.

My response is to actively consider what your concerns are. Don’t keep just thinking about them or, worse, blanking them out. 

Then take the plunge and seek help.

Ignoring or denying your concerns can turn them into a vicious circle of tension and stress which then leads to a worsening of your physical and mental health. I call this the cycle of suffering. 

Therapy with me can break this cycle by getting to the root cause of your fears and identifying ways to address them. I can give you tools and techniques to not only cope, but also move forward. To regain control of your life and feel like the person you want to be.

Sometimes in life, you just have to go for it.

And when you do, the best things can happen.

A recovered chronic pain patient herself, Claire utilizes Pain Reprocessing Therapy (PRT), Integrative Somatic Parts Work (similar to IFS), Focusing, Gestalt, and a range of other evidence-based modalities to foster deep, lasting change. Having witnessed the transformational impact of her own recovery, she dedicates her work to supporting others through their healing processes.

I provide therapy to patients in Colorado, where I am licensed, including teletherapy across CO and walk-and-talk therapy in Denver, CO. I provide coaching to clients worldwide.

Joanne Lee Smith, RN (Child) / SCPHN – IWS

Neuroplastic Recovery Nurse Coach
Joanne started nursing children in the NHS in 2001, this later evolved to working in the  community as a Specialist Public Health Nurse (Health Visitor) supporting families from  2008 - 2022.
In 2008 a car accident sparked 16 years of chronic pain. Mainstream fixes failed, even  after becoming a long-distance trail runner and achieve 100-mile ultra marathons while  fit and strong she did not recover and was diagnosed with fibromyalgia and later ADHD  and other neuroplastic symptoms.
In 2018 she trained as a coach as part of her Nursing role and began coaching others  trail runners who also suffered with chronic health issues and symptoms.  A breakthrough occurred during her Innate Wellbeing Practitioner training, she learned  about Dr. Sarno's approach, and she implemented it 6 months later and symptoms  began to disappear gradually over time.
Along with 27 years of experience in health care (NHS) & currently working part time as  clinical lead nurse, she is also a Professional Coach & Mentor, Innate Wellbeing  Specialist, Holistic Therapies Practitioner, NLP Master Practitioner. She is also a  practitioner in 3 Principles Coaching.
Her passion lies in supporting active adults and children of all ages to overcome chronic  pain and symptoms, safely returning to full activity. Through personalized coaching  rooted in neuroplasticity, and guiding lasting recovery.

I was born in South Africa but have been living in the UK since 2000. With a Master’s degree in Osteopathy and over 25 years of experience in movement, posture, and alignment—including teaching Reformer Pilates—I have dedicated my career to understanding pain, the body, and how we heal.

As a dedicated sportsman myself, with a particular interest in running and triathlon, I understand the pain and symptoms others experience during these activities. Using my knowledge and extensive background in anatomy I understand why people relate their pain to structural changes in their body and how to support them.

Early in my journey, I focused on physical treatments, but over time, I realised that true, lasting recovery often lies beyond the physical. My own experience with injuries, as well as my extensive study of pain science, led me to adopt a more holistic, mind-body approach. Through my Neuroplastic Pain treatments and the SIRPA (Stress-Induced Pain Recovery Association) Programme, I have seen first-hand how shifting away from purely structural treatments towards an evidence-based psychological approach can help people achieve lasting relief.

As a SIRPA-certified practitioner, I specialise in helping people overcome persistent pain and other neuroplastic symptoms, whether they present as back pain or any other structural pains as well as migraines, digestive issues, fibromyalgia, chronic fatigue, tinnitus. My work is influenced by leading pain science experts, including Dr John Sarno, Dr Howard Schubiner, and Dr David Hanscom, whose DOC (Direct Your Own Care) Method provides a structured approach to rewiring neural pathways and addressing the mind-body connection in recovery.

I have been practising medicine for over 35 years, and as an Integrative Consultant Psychiatrist for over 25 years. I am a highly specialised and experienced doctor with a broad range of expertise and clinical skills in General Practice, General Adult and Elderly Care Psychiatry. I have always had a curious and inquisitive mindset, especially about what I consider to be the blind spots in Western Medicine, specifically:

  • Chronic Primary Pain and Medically Unexplained Symptoms (MUS) and the inadequate and often myopic approach to these areas by Western Medicine, where patients are most frequently subjected to medical gaslighting.

  • Undiagnosed and untreated ADHD, particularly overfocussed ADD in women.

  • Female hormonal mental health issues in the perimenopause and mesopause

  • The impact of mental health issues on the autonomic nervous system, disconnection the fracturing of sense of self

I am a mind-body and trauma informed expert clinician, who uses a biopsychosocial and evidence-based approach to Chronic Primary Pain, Chronic Functional Syndromes, and medically unexplained symptoms. My special interests and areas of expertise are:

  • Chronic Primary Pain (neuroplastic pain) and Mindbody Medicine (Psychophysiologic Disorders)

  • Undiagnosed and untreated ADHD and ADD

  • Female Hormonal Mental Health

  • Psychedelic Assisted Therapy and

  • The role of Cannabis Based Medicinal Products in Mental Health.

  • The Dysfunctional Autonomic Nervous system

I am the only UK based Medical Doctor (Physician) and Consultant Psychiatrist skilled in the diagnostic assessment and treatment of these conditions and all associated comorbid mental health conditions.

I have recovered from neuroplastic pain myself -https://www.thepainrecoveryprogram.com/blog/my-story-wayne

I am a member of the International Association for the Study of Pain (IASP), the World Institute of Pain and The British Pain Society.

I coordinated and chaired The Pain Edition of the Masters Series in February 2023 https://themasterseries.com/pain-edition-2023/ ; https://www.drwaynekampers.co.uk/blogs-articles-podcasts/the-masters-series-pain-editiond and chaired the panel on Chronic Primary Pain in the Sheldonian Theatre at The Masters Series Oxford at the end of September 2023 https://www.drwaynekampers.co.uk/blogs-articles-podcasts/the-masters-series-oxford

It was an honour to be invited to chair the panel discussion on Chronic Primary Pain and share the stage with Dr Stephen Porges, Dr Susan Carter, and Dr Mel Pohl

I am a member of Multidisciplinary Association for Psychedelic Studies and a certified Ketamine Assisted Therapist.

I am the Consultant lead for 2 Intravenous Ketamine Assisted Therapy Clinics in London and also offer Ketamine Assisted Therapy using oral Ketamine Lozenges https://www.drwaynekampers.co.uk/ketamine-assisted-therapy-kat

I am a member of the Medical Cannabis Clinicians Society and the Medicinal Institute of Education and have had expert medical cannabis training specifically for UK doctors.

You can contact me via my website. https://www.drwaynekampers.co.uk/contact

column22:
My name is Chad Volk and I am a psychologically informed physical therapist with a passion for helping people overcome pain and dysfunction. I earned my undergraduate Mathematics degree and doctorate of physical therapy (DPT) degree from the University of Vermont in 2011. I also earned my orthopedic specialist certification (OCS) in 2019.

I have been practicing outpatient physical therapy full time since 2011. The chief complaint I treat in the clinic is pain and it has become chronic (>3 months) for most of my patients. I’ve now been specializing in treating chronic pain for the last seven years. Awareness of the mind-body connection led me to Pain Reprocessing Therapy and I now use it with all patients in some capacity. Physical Therapy practice allows me to be eective and eficient in ruling in or out structural vs. neuroplastic pain disorders.


It is always my focus to help patients manage pain, maximize function, and reach each individual goal while empowering them with the tools to self-manage their condition and prevent future recurrence. As a clinician I practice extensively in orthopedics, sports medicine, balance and functional retraining, vestibular rehab, persistent pain, and concussion rehab.
By appreciating the power and importance of evidence based medicine and active listening, I strive to consistently provide compassionate and comprehensive care to each and every patient. My goal is to build long-term, lasting relationships with my patients, and to treat every patient as if they are part of my family.

He specializes in the treatment of chronic pain, anxiety (including OCD), depression, and sexuality. He earned both a masters degree in Social Work and Public Administration from the University of Southern California. column22:
I am an Adult and Geriatric Nurse Practitioner with over 20 years of experience in hospice and palliative care. I found that traditional pain treatments are very effective for pain caused by structural problems. Yet for many patients, especially those with chronic pain, traditional pain treatments were not working, and were actually harmful. I felt frustrated that there were many patients whose needs I could not meet. These patients’ symptoms did not fit the traditional medical model of disease diagnosis and treatment. I knew that I needed to find a new way to help these clients.


My search led me to find two breakthrough evidence-based treatment models: 1) Pain Reprocessing Therapy, and 2) Emotional Awareness and Expression Therapy. I'm excited because now I have an effective way to meet more patients’ needs, particularly those considered “difficult to treat.”

Since training in these two techniques, I feel I’ve found the missing puzzle piece. Finally I have reliable, evidence-based tools to combine with my nursing skills and years of clinical expertise. In partnership with my patients, we are able to better explain the source of the formerly
unexplained pain and symptoms.

I am a graduate of the Columbia University School of Nursing, and have advanced certification in geriatrics and hospice and palliative care. I hold a Certificate in Bioethics and Medical Humanities from the Montefiore-Einstein Center for Bioethics. In my spare time I am an amateur tree climber and arborist.

column22:
David is a psychotherapist (Licensed in Florida). He specializes in working with adults who have chronic pain (mind-body, TMS, PPD, etc.) that is medically unexplained, such as back/neck pain and Fibromyalgia, and that is not getting better with conventional medical/physical treatments. Research has shown that this type of chronic pain may be caused by emotional and psychological factors, which can include past childhood trauma. Having personally experienced this type of chronic pain, and recovered from it, he is able to understand and empathize, on a deeper level, with his clients who may be struggling with this issue.

He is also a Certified Clinical Trauma Specialist and specializes in working with adults who have experienced childhood trauma, or Adverse Childhood Experiences (ACEs).  He is trained in EMDR, Pain Reprocessing Therapy, and in Forward Facing Trauma Therapy. These treatments and approaches have proven to be very effective in helping people heal from the effects of their childhood trauma, decrease their symptoms of anxiety and depression, and improve their overall quality of life. He strongly believes in the mind-body connection and in a holistic approach to counseling/coaching.

PPDA Board Member / ATNS Board Member

A former mind body symptom sufferer himself – in the form of eight years of seemingly chronic back pain – Dr. Ratner is an experienced psychologist specializing in the treatment of mind body sufferers. Dr. Ratner is a member of the PPDA board, has a professional relationship with many of the medical doctors in the field, including Dr. John Stracks, Dr. David Schechter, Dr. Dave Clarke, Dr. David Hanscom, and Dr. Ira Rashbaum, and was a supervisee of TMS therapist and author Eric Sherman, PsyD at the start of his work in the field. In describing his work, Dr. Ratner writes, “I work collaboratively with patients to get them information on how to understand mind body experience, understand and organize their emotional worlds, reduce doubt that they can get better, and expand their sense of control over symptoms and their own life and self-concept. I often in a more relaxed manner, using humor, warmth, and openness to build strong connections.” Dr. Ratner’s Crushing Doubt podcast – available on Youtube and Spotify –is an additional resource for mind body sufferers.

I am a Registered Clinical Counsellor (RCC #9099) and a member in good standing with the British Columbia Association of Clinical Counsellors (BCACC). I received my Master’s degree in Clinical Psychology (Marriage, Family, and Child Counselling) in 1999 from Pepperdine University in Los Angeles, CA, and a PhD in Medical Sciences in 2008 from Poznan University of Medical Sciences, Poland.

I am fascinated by the brain and body connection and the immense possibilities that arise when we open ourselves to learning and understanding how various psychoneurobiological mechanisms work, how our environments and circumstances have shaped us and continue to do so, and how we can harness the power of that understanding towards change. I specialize in working with people living with chronic/persistent pain, fibromyalgia, chronic fatigue syndrome and other psychophysiologic disorders, PTSD, anxiety and depression.

My overriding clinical orientation honours: the mind-body connection, neurobiology, somatic, experiential, psychodynamic, existential-humanistic approaches, and the bio-psycho-socio- spiritual nature of our lives. I use variety of approaches such as Pain Reprocessing Therapy, Sensorimotor and Somatic Experiencing Therapies, Eye Movement Desensitization and Reprocessing Therapy (EMDR), Accelerated Experiential Dynamic Psychotherapy (AEDP), Neurobiological Approach, Emotion-Focused Therapy, Applied Polyvagal Theory and others.

Dr. Antje Kallweit is a German physician, board-certified in Anesthesiology with additional certification in Special Pain Therapy. She specializes in the diagnosis and treatment of chronic primary and nociplastic pain conditions.


Her clinical work focuses on neuroplastic pain mechanisms, integrating contemporary pain neuroscience, Pain Reprocessing Therapy (PRT), and Emotional Awareness and Expression Therapy (EAET) into a structured, biopsychosocial treatment approach. She has received training and ongoing professional exchange with experienced clinicians in this field, including Howard Schubiner.


Dr. Kallweit is the founder and medical lead of HELP, a digital therapeutic program for chronic primary pain that translates neuroplastic pain principles into a structured, evidence-based treatment pathway. Alongside her digital health work, she continues to provide clinical care within the German statutory health insurance system (KV), offering telehealth services to patients in Germany.

column22:
Few things disrupt a person’s life more than being in constant pain. And dealing with a chronic illness can be so devastating to your plans and hopes and dreams – it changes everything. While medical treatment is your doctor’s job, there is always a psychological component to physical issues. If you have a chronic illness, you may feel depressed and demoralized, and it’s tough for your body to fight if your mind doesn’t have enough energy. It is now abundantly clear from research that mind/body interactions affect the course of illnesses.

Similarly, pain always has a mental side. If you focus on your pain, it’s going to hurt more. And some psychological issues can also increase pain, causing PPD/TMS (Tension Myoneural Syndrome). It’s very important to understand that, even if there is a mental component to your pain, this doesn’t mean that the pain is not real. When I help people with chronic pain, I don’t minimize the experience of the pain or doubt that it hurts. Rather, I look for ways that your body may be expressing something on behalf of your mind. I am influenced by the work of Dr. John Sarno with back pain. My patients have had great success in overcoming back and neck pain, as well as other issues such as irritable bowel syndrome, chronic fatigue, fibromyalgia, and bursitis using his techniques and theories.

Drawing from multiple modalities, I am able to tailor a treatment plan customized to address your specific concerns and personal goals.
 
In a warm, safe and supportive space, together we collaborate to examine current challenges, achieve insight and understanding, enhance self-awareness, identify strengths, and create new proactive approaches to help you heal, grow, and thrive.

Welcome! I am glad that you are here. I can help you recover from chronic pain and chronic conditions of all sorts through new education about modern neuroscience and a host of innovate techniques.  I believe in supporting the whole person: body, mind and spirit. With self-reflection, education and curiosity, while becoming aware of the wisdom of our physical bodies, we hold great power to enact change and growth.  So if you are feeling hopeless about your situation, please know that there are many things we can do to keep moving forward.

I have been on my own personal journey through neuroplastic symptoms focused primarily on multiple autoimmune issues that led to a rainbow of symptoms.  I have found this approach to be transformational.

 I am a clinical psychologist who has been practicing psychotherapy for more than 35 years. I hold a Masters in Counseling Psychology and a Doctorate degree in Clinical Psychology.  I have training in pain reprocessing therapy (PRT), emotional awareness and expression therapy (EAET), emotionally focused therapies (EFT), and clinical Brainspotting for clearing out traumas of all kinds. These methods can help you heal and develop a new relationship with yourself. Please reach out to find out more.

Caley is a psychotherapist with an interest in human behaviour, trauma, chronic stress and mood disorders. She specializes in the area of chronic pain and chronic illness. She retrained as a psychotherapist after working as a chiropractor for 13 years. Over the course of her clinical career Caley became increasingly aware of the need for mental health professionals with an expertise in the intersection of mental and physical health. She has a special interest in supporting front line workers and postpartum women. She is trained in the following techniques: Cognitive Behavioural Therapy (CBT), Acceptance and Commitment Therapy (ACT) and Pain Reprocessing Therapy (PRT) and Motivational Interviewing (MI). Caley is trauma informed and welcomes members of the LGBTQ2+ as well as the neurodivergent communities.

David D. Clarke, MD is President of the Association for Treatment of Neuroplastic Symptoms. He is also Assistant Director at the Center for Ethics and Clinical Assistant Professor of Gastroenterology Emeritus both at Oregon Health & Science University (OHSU) in Portland, Oregon, USA. He is board-certified in Gastroenterology and Internal Medicine and practiced Gastroenterology in Portland from 1984 to 2009. During that time, he diagnosed and treated over 7000 patients whose symptoms were not explained by organ disease or injury.

Dr. Clarke has lectured extensively on Neuroplastic Symptoms to health care professionals, graduate students, and the public across North America and in Europe. His book for patients, They Can’t Find Anything Wrong!, was praised by a president of the American Psychosomatic Society as “truly remarkable” and named one of the five best mind-body books ever written by Brit & Co. He was the lead editor for the professional textbook Psychophysiologic Disorders and the companion textbook A Diagnostic Guide for Psychophysiologic Disorders.

He has done hundreds of interviews for TV, Radio, and podcasts and was an Executive Producer of three documentary films about Neuroplastic Symptoms.

column22:
PPDA Board Member / ATNS Board Member

David Schechter, MD is in private practice in Culver City (LA), California and on staff at Cedars-Sinai Medical Center.  He has been a Clinical Associate Professor at USC School of Medicine, currently inactive. He is Board Certified in Family Medicine, Sports Medicine, and was a Diplomate of the Academy of Integrative Pain Medicine

Dr. Schechter has been familiar with the mind-body method and healing program since 1982 and has personally examined and diagnosed over three thousand patients with this disorder in his private practice. He has achieved a high rate of treatment success according to two follow-up studies he has done in his office, one published in a peer-reviewed journal. 

Dr. Schechter was first a patient and then a student of Dr. John Sarno while a medical student at NYU School of Medicine in the early 1980’s. He wrote The MindBody Workbook as the first guided journal specific to this field. He is the author of several books, articles, and chapters in this area of medicine including Think Away Your Pain, and The MindBody Workbook for Teens.

 

Books: The MindBody Workbook (1999), Think Away Your Pain (2014), The MindBody Workbook for Teens (2019), Psychophysiologic Disorders: Trauma Informed, Interprofessional Diagnosis & Treatment (2019)

I am Elizabeth Curet, a licensed mental health counselor with a master’s degree in addiction and mental health counseling. I own and operate Nothing Is Impossible Counseling, LLC, use Accelerated Experiential Dynamic and trained with AEDP supervisors for more than five years, achieving Level III. I also guide people who experience emotionally painful shame reaching certification as a Certified Healing Shame Practitioner. I have also completed the initial 21-hr course in Pain Reprocessing Therapy in September 2025.


I specialize in anxiety, depression, shame, relational trauma, chronic pain, and non-pain symptoms, sometimes known as neuroplastic symptoms. I also work with couples and one or both of the couple experiences chronic pain/symptoms, which we also support and guide these patients to reduce and/or reverse their symptoms.

I have worked in substance use treatment facilities in the south Florida area from 2014- 2018.

With experience in evidence-based modalities such as Accelerated Experiential Dynamic Psychotherapy (AEDP), Pain Reprocessing Therapy (PRT), and mindfulness, Elizabeth works to shift patients’/clients’ understanding and behavior towards pain, while assisting and empowering individuals toward healing their history of Adverse Childhood Experiences (ACE), relationship trauma and past/present stressors. I work with clients to improve overall well-being inside and out, and offer to integrate faith- based interventions and prayer for clients requesting them.

I am passionate about helping individuals with chronic pain, and non-pain symptoms, as I struggled with them for 20+ years myself. My patients and I have found healing, significance, and resilience in our lives and relationships as a result of working with chronic symptoms and trauma.

Personalized Virtual Coaching for Pelvic Pain Recovery

Struggling with persistent pelvic, abdominal, or intimate-area pain—and not sure where to turn next?

Having symptoms such as burning, itching, heaviness, PGAD, sexual pain, urinary urgency or bowel elimination issues?

I’m Evelyn Hecht, PT, a pelvic pain specialist with 28+ years of experience helping people with conditions like Vulvodynia, Pudendal Neuralgia, Interstitial Cystitis, PGAD, IBS, constipation, and pelvic floor dysfunction find effective, lasting relief.

Through private virtual coaching sessions (HIPAA-compliant via Zoom), I help you:

🔹 Understand what could be happening in your nervous system and pelvic floor region

🔹 Learn self-regulation strategies grounded in pain neuroscience to reduce pelvic muscle tension

🔹 Address symptoms like burning, urgency, heaviness, and discomfort

🔹 Feel less fear and more in control of your healing path

🔹 Learn a gentle progression of body movements to reduce pelvic distress

🔹 Practice strategies to optimize bladder, bowel, sexual, and core stabilization functions

🔹 Receive expert guidance that complements your medical care

This is not psychotherapy or physical therapy—it’s expert pelvic pain coaching rooted in the biopsychosocial model. My approach is collaborative, trauma-informed, and designed to meet you where you are.

✅ Suitable for all genders

✅ Fully virtual—no physical exams or internal assessments

✅ Optional home practice resources available (e.g., PelvicSense)

📅 Ready to feel seen, supported, and equipped to move forward? Visit www.pelvicsense.com to learn more or schedule your first virtual session.

column20: Clinical Ayurvedic Specialist and Neuroplastic Symptom Recovery Coach
column22:
Hello!
I offer a very accommodating sliding scale.
I am passionate about all things that help people feel well: physically, mentally, spiritually, and I believe this is the place we must start for a healthy, peaceful planet. Ayurveda, yoga and the natural world have taught me invaluable lessons that help me feel my best, so I can be who I want to be. I would feel truly honored to share these tools with you and to support you on your journey.
In 1995, I shattered my pelvis and back in a paragliding accident and spent a year in a wheelchair. Two years after my accident, I experienced 18 months of severe nerve pain, which was misdiagnosed as a result of my crooked pelvis, fused sacrum and herniated discs. When I came across Healing Back Pain, I learned that my pain was not due to my structural imperfections, but rather repressed emotions. I began to truly heal and 30 years later I am 100% pain free and an avid rock climber, surfer, skier and biker.
I have been sharing tools to help clients treat their Mind Body Symptoms for over 25 years. I would be honored to support you on your journey.

column20: Licensed Clinical Mental Health Counselor - Psychotherapist
column22:
Because suffering with chronic health problems is a daily struggle, I believe that access to daily support makes a huge difference in recovery. To that end, I offer Chronic To Cured, an intensive mentorship-style approach to healing that facilitates the quickest possible relief from neuroplastic symptoms through access to substantive daily support directly from me. I provide specific instruction on how to apply Pain Reprocessing Therapy to your unique situation, but more importantly, I help you discover the often hard-to-find root causes of why you developed chronic symptoms to begin with. I provide comprehensive teaching on the interplay between complex, interrelated factors that help to create mind-body symptoms in a systematic, organized way, and then I give you very specific, tailored guidance on how to address them.

Because of my training as a psychotherapist, my approach has been particularly helpful for those who are currently using Pain Reprocessing Therapy (PRT) and the mind-body healing model, but have still haven’t experienced complete resolution of symptoms. I comprehensively address the developmental factors that set you up with a tendency toward the behavioral patterns that fuel neuroplastic symptoms. I am particularly astute at unearthing the unconscious factors that fuel resistance and keep you stuck even when using PRT diligently.

In addition to daily support directly from me, you have access to a small community of others who are struggling with similar symptoms and working with me. Covered topics include: the basics of pain science, neuroplasticity, and principles of brain training; techniques for processing emotions, including the use of energy psychology modalities; how to address the shame that underlies and fosters neuroplastic symptoms; psychodynamic patterns in childhood that set you up for symptoms; strategies for changing negative cognitive and emotional patterns; various methods for soothing the nervous system; and insights into the symbolism that is embedded in many symptoms that can serve as pointers to lifestyle adjustments that need to be made.

I suffered for 25 years with various symptoms before I found the mind-body model for healing neuroplastic symptoms, so I am passionate about helping others avoid the same detours and setbacks I experienced.

I am deeply empathic, and draw on my intuitive skills honed while learning energy healing methods when working with clients. I have been practicing as a Licensed Clinical Mental Health Counselor since 2003 and love to work with complex cases. I see individuals and couples in my private psychotherapy practice.

I have particular expertise in treating dissociative disorders and trauma, using Energy Psychology methods to resolve all forms of anxiety, as well as compulsive thoughts and behaviors. My work is informed by Polyvagal Theory, Internal Family Systems Theory, and my longstanding personal study and practice of mindfulness. I specialize in integrating spirituality into the therapy (when desired by the client) and I am particularly skilled in helping clients resolve the shame-based thinking that undergirds and promotes neuroplastic symptoms.

Note: All services are virtual and available worldwide.

column22:
Encarna Espunya (Valencia, Spain 1959)

I’ve been a health care professional for more than 30 years, beginning as a Spinologist, I trained in the United States in the 1980’s. I was one of the first graduates of the Philadelphia Spinal Tutorium. Later, in Spain, I was trained as a Physical Therapist and Osteopath, in the University of Valencia. Also, for some years I was an associate professor of this same University in the Physical Therapy Department; I was also an associate professor for a time in the CEU (Cardenal Herrera University) in Valencia. For more than 15 years I trained numerous health care professionals in the techniques of Spinology.

I’ve also been trained in Pediatric Physical Therapy, and participate actively as a volunteer advisor in NGO’s in the area of the disabled. During all this time, I have worked exclusively in the treatment and care of the spine and muscle-skeletal pain in my clinics, first in Madrid and now in Valencia and Barcelona. (Spain). In performing my work, I realized that although I have provided much relief to my patients, my work (just like other manual therapies such as osteopathy and chiropractic, etc.) generates a certain dependence: in the majority of the cases, the patient had to return regularly in order to maintain the relief obtained. A definitive cure was not achieved, except on some occasions. Since I am passionate about this work, I have stayed attentive to everything that “came out” new that could help give my patients (and myself) a definitive solution.

From this point, I came into contact with the so-called ‘Stress Disorders’ and the ‘Psychophysiologic Disorders’, the Tension Myoneural Syndrome (TMS) and the Mind-Body Syndrome. Hence, my qualification in London in the S.I.R.P.A. Training Program. There I learned the application of specialized and specific programs for the treatment of chronic pain and chronic diseases. These have been years of study and preparation, being trained and looking for the best professional in this field at this time. From Georgie Oldfield, himself, creator and developer of SIRPA, derived from the research and work of the North American rehabilitator/physician, John E.  Sarno.

In England, I had the opportunity to know and study with: Howard Schubiner, David Clarke, David Hanscom, in the first Chronic Pain Conference. And in Spain through the Sociedad Española de Fisioterapia y Dolor, SEFID, [Spanish Physical Therapy and Pain Society], authors at the level of the Australian physical therapists such as Peter O’Sullivan or Lorimer Moseley, the latter, the author of the book ‘Explain Pain’, are among my influences and affiliates.

The Chronic Pain Recovery Program, within the SIRPA approach that I offer in my clinic is the result of years of work, research and experience that has led me to seek the most effective strategies in the world for such purpose.

column22:
Evan Loukusa, MD is a physician practicing in the beautiful surroundings of Michigan’s Upper Peninsula. Board certified in Family Medicine, he is trained in Pain Reprocessing Therapy and pursuing further training in Emotional Awareness and Expression Therapy. With his background in emergency room and hospital settings, he is well-equipped to rule out structural disorders and establish a firm diagnosis of neuroplastic symptoms where appropriate.


Dr. Loukusa became interested in treating neuroplastic symptoms through his own recovery from chronic low back pain. Where he once hobbled through hospital corridors, he now enjoys mountain biking and hiking with his family. He strives to hear every patient’s story and help them understand how recovery from debilitating symptoms is possible.


After graduating from medical school at the University of Minnesota, Dr. Loukusa completed his residency training at UP Health Systems-Marquette Family Medicine Residency and practices hospital, emergency, and outpatient medicine at a critical access hospital in L’anse, Michigan. Other clinical interests include substance use treatment, lifestyle medicine, and clinical hypnosis.

Eric G. Sherman, Psy.D., a graduate of The New York University Postdoctoral Program in Psychotherapy and Psychoanalysis has a full-time private practice in New York. He also provides supervision and training to mental health professionals.  Since completing his clinical psychology internship in 1984 at Rusk Institute—NYU Langone Medical Center under the aegis of the late John Sarno, MD, Dr. Sherman has specialized in the assessment and treatment of mindbody disorders, as well as the psychological issues associated with serious medical illness and disability. Together with Dr. Frances S. Anderson, Dr. Sherman co-authored “Pathways to Pain Relief” (2013) which details how treatment for psychophysiologic pain disorders works from the perspective of both the patient and the therapist.  Dr. Sherman is a founding Board Member of the PPDA (Psychophysiologic Disorders Association) and was recently appointed Co-chair of the Committee on Health Care and Psychoanalysis of Division 39.  Along with Dr. Anderson, he teaches a course, "When Stress Causes Pain, Can the Psychoanalyst Intervene?" at the Manhattan Institute for Psychoanalysis in NYC.

Note: Washington State services include in-person & virtual counseling. Idaho services are limited to virtual counseling only. Virtual PPD coaching is available in other states on a case-by-case basis.

Erik Bohlin, MA is a licensed counselor in Washington and Idaho and has been in private practice and has been counseling and coaching for 25+ years. His personal experience as a PPD suffer drove him to seek out solutions to learn how to express his feelings better, develop better boundaries around self-care and resolve childhood trauma. Erik works with chronic pain, addiction, trauma and anxiety. Erik uses principles from NLP, Ericksonian Hypnosis and Neurofeedback. He received training with Dr. Howard Schubiner, Alan Gordon, MSW, and Hal Greenham, BSc. In working with chronic pain sufferers, Erik likes to use a number of approaches: Emotional Awareness and Expression Therapy (EAET), Internal Family Systems and is certified in Pain Reprocessing Therapy through the Pain Psychology Clinic (Alan Gordon). He believes that through expressive writing, somatic tracking and do internal parts work, the client can begin to see the link between their pain and their emotions. He is both certified as Nationally Certified Counselor (NBCC) as well as a Master's Addiction Counselor (NAADAC).

I also spent a year as a visiting scholar at NYU before my residency education. I am committed to staying updated on the latest advances and best practices in this rapidly evolving specialty, and to educating and empowering my patients and colleagues on the importance of addressing orofacial pain and sleep issues.

column22:
Psychotherapy for complex trauma and somatic symptoms
Specializing in Intensive Short Term Dynamic Psychotherapy

Eveline Wu, Licensed Marriage and Family Therapist, brings 20 years working with people with chronic pain who tried extensively other holistic approaches, using the Feldenkrais Method of Somatic Education. In the course of that work, which brings detailed understanding of how movement can address the body patterns of anxiety, & great outcomes, she became interested in complementary somatic therapy approaches, eventually becoming a Licensed Marriage and Family Therapist. She has studied many approaches of somatic psychotherapy approaches for trauma. Her continual seeking the best outcomes for her patients led her to discover Intensive Short Term Dynamic Psychotherapy, an integrative approach for addressing emotional issues underlying stress based health and pain disorders, in which she currently trains and practices.

Wu sees clients online in California, and can be available for consultation or referral for other locations. She maintains a practice which is inclusive and accepting of racial, sexual and gender diversity, and the effect of sociocultural stress on the body.

column22: I have been treating people in pain for a very long time (since 1990) and have been seeking answers as to the optimal treatment approach this whole time. It has been the driver for me to learn more and I have never stopped. I like to think I have been aware of the major changes in treatment approaches before they become mainstream, and this is certainly the case with mindbody conditions. Although these have been recognised by some for a long time (Dr Sarno), mindbody conditions mimicking musculoskeletal problems are not widely treated as such within my profession of Physiotherapy. The orthodox perspective acknowledges the effect that the psyche can have on pain but it falls short of recognising the role the mind has in generating the pain disorders. As a physio with a strong background in manual and manipulative therapy and exercise rehabilitation I can understand this scepticism, I had it myself, but having been exposed to the powers of the mind to generate debilitating pain and then alleviate it, I no longer have doubt. I feel like Neo in the Matrix, having taken the red pill and being shown the truth, there is now no going back to the old way. I have been practicing Cognitive Functional Therapy (CFT) since its foundation having meticulously followed Prof Peter O’Sullivan and his colleagues and have been following the work promoted by SIRPA and colleagues since my training in 2017, which seems to complement the CFT really well. I completed the Pain Reprocessing Therapy Practitioners course in 2020, which gave me further insight into the amazing ability of the brain to recover. As part of this journey I have now qualified as a hypnotherapist.

I am a non-medical practitioner of psychotherapy (Heilpraktikerin für Psychotherapie) in Hamburg, Germany, certified by the Hamburg City Health Board. I specialize in Pain Reprocessing Therapy (PRT), Emotional Awareness and Expression Therapy (EAET), and JournalSpeak, following the Sarno-Sachs Solution program. I offer these approaches primarily in German, with English available upon request.

In addition to being a member of the ATNS (formerly PPDA), I am also a member of the Association of Independent Psychotherapists, Alternative Practitioners for Psychotherapy, and Psychological Consultants (Verband Freier Psychotherapeuten, Heilpraktiker für Psychotherapie und Psychologischer Berater e.V.) in Germany.

My approach to clients with PPD symptoms combines psychoeducation on brain science with elements of PRT, somatic tracking, emotional processing with EAET, and JournalSpeak. I also incorporate relaxation exercises, guided meditations, and breathwork to support the nervous system regulation. Together with the client, I explore PPD-related personality traits—how they have served them in the past and how they may have contributed to their symptoms. We also examine childhood wounds and adverse childhood experiences (ACEs).

I help clients break the pain-fear cycle by restructuring thought patterns and reprocessing their symptoms on cognitive, emotional, and nervous system levels, leading to more agency, flexibility and lightness. One of my most important goals as a therapist is to guide clients in applying mind-body tools with self-love, empathy, patience, and kindness toward themselves.

Dr. Hannah Holmes, PhD, is a licensed psychologist specializing in evidence-based therapies for chronic pain, anxiety, and related psychophysiological disorders. In addition to individual psychotherapy, Dr. Holmes leads an 8-week virtual Pain Reprocessing Therapy Group, focused on retraining the brain's response to chronic pain. I am a clinical social worker (LCSW) and psychotherapist licensed in CT and D.C. I specialize in treating chronic pain and other mind-body symptoms using Pain Reprocessing Therapy, Emotional Awareness and Expression Therapy, Acceptance and Commitment Therapy as well as other modalities. My own journey with chronic pain has deepened my empathy, shaped my therapeutic approach, and reinforced my commitment to a compassionate, holistic practice that honors each client’s unique healing process.

I have expertise assisting clients overcoming pelvic, back and neck pain, headaches as well as other issues such as irritable bowel syndrome, fibromyalgia, and long Covid. Christina P. Kantzavelos, LCSW, is a licensed psychotherapist@beginwithintoday, award-winning gluten-free and health-conscious food, travel and lifestyle writer @buenqamino, and Author of the Begin Within Today Healing Journals, for chronic illness and mental wellness.  She received both her BA and MSW from the University of California Los Angeles (UCLA) and her MLIS from San Jose State University (SJSU). 

She specializes in treating clients with chronic health conditions (aka spoonies and medical refugees) and trauma (including medical trauma). Chronic medical conditions (i.e. PPD, diabetes, dysautonomia, EDS, Lyme disease, Bartonella, Hashimoto’s, mast cell activation syndrome, vasculitis, back, and neck pain, fibromyalgia, chronic fatigue syndrome, vertigo, IBS, IBD, sickle cell disease, mold illness, etc.) often come with significant challenges, like fear, anxiety, medical PTSD, and may interfere with relationships and daily function. It is her experience and belief that chronic illnesses are often rooted in trauma(s) that cause a chronic stress/fight-or-flight response, not allowing the body to prioritize healing. She utilizes various holistic evidence-based treatment modalities and practice-based evidence such as EMDR to get to the root, with an added focus on mindfulness techniques, positive psychology, neural retraining, and strength’s-based therapy.  In addition, she provides life coaching, case management, and elder and disability care planning and support.

column22:
Pippa graduated from the University of Southampton, England, UK with a Master’s degree in Audiology (MSc) in 2003 and began work as an audiologist for the National Health Service (NHS), specialising in hearing assessment and rehabilitation. A few years later she started experiencing chronic dizziness symptoms and was diagnosed with chronic vestibular migraine. She was largely unable to work for the next nine years, and her life was very limited during this time. After trying many medications, vestibular rehabilitation exercises and diets with limited success, she began to read chronic pain research and books about new approaches to treating chronic conditions and started applying them to her dizziness. She was able to recover fully and get back to leading a normal life without symptoms in 2016. She returned to work as an audiologist and has since successfully used the techniques she used to recover to help people with chronic dizziness (caused by Persistent Postural-Perceptual Dizziness, vestibular migraine and Mal de Débarquement Syndrome), hyperacusis, and problematic tinnitus. Pippa is certified as a Holistic Life Coach, has studied vestibular rehabilitation with Hypatia Training and is trained in Pain Reprocessing Therapy as
well as completing the Freedom From Chronic Pain course. She is trained in Accelerated Experiential Dynamic Psychotherapy (AEDP) and has completed Level 2 training in this. She currently sees people for private, online video appointments whilst continuing to work part-time for the NHS as a specialist Audiologist. She is passionate about helping others who are struggling with chronic dizziness, tinnitus, hyperacusis and misophonia to recover from these debilitating conditions. She lives in England with her husband, two children and two cats.

Dr. Schubiner is an internist and a Clinical Professor at the Michigan State University College of Human Medicine and is a fellow in the American College of Physicians, and the American Academy of Pediatrics. He has authored more than 100 publications in scientific journals and books, and lectures regionally, nationally, and internationally. He has conducted several research studies in the application of mind-body medicine techniques for fibromyalgia and back pain. These randomized, controlled trials used pain reprocessing therapy and emotional awareness and expression therapy. They showed that these treatments are more effective than currently used pain therapies.

Dr. Schubiner has consulted for the American Medical Association, the National Institute on Drug Abuse, and the National Institute on Mental Health. Dr. Schubiner is the author of three books: Unlearn Your Pain, Unlearn Your Anxiety and Depression, and Hidden From View, written with Dr. Allan Abbass. He lives in the Detroit area with his wife of forty years and has two adult children. His website is UnlearnYourPain.com.

I have been practicing as a psychologist for 31 years. While working as a rehabilitation psychologist at the Rusk Institute of Rehabilitation Medicine in New York City, I was introduced to Dr. John Sarno, with whom I was unfamiliar at the time. Dr. Sarno trained me as a "TMS" psychologist, to which it was then referred. My practice changed dramatically as I was fortunate enough to be able to integrate this new philosophy of mind/body healing, now referred to as the Treatment of Neuroplastic Symptoms, into my practice.

I have been excited to see the changes in peoples' lives as a result of this treatment philosophy and the ways in which therapy has expanded in its methodologies.  In my current practice, I have seen and continue to see people from all parts of the world who have been exposed to this treatment modality. I have seen peoples' lives change dramatically. In large part, this has been rendered possible through the availability of telehealth. 

Prior to my career as a psychologist, I worked in the corporate world. I often regret that I didn't have the knowledge or skills then to apply to myself and coworkers.
