column20: TMS MindBody, Neuroplastic Solutions, PRT, Somatic Methods, Fitness & Rehab
column22:
Dr Tovah Goldfine is a practicing Chiropractor and Specialist in Fitness and Rehabilitation for 40 years...  With this knowledge she has been able to help hundreds of patients out of pain and back on their feet moving again in a regular exercise routine.

During Dr Tovah’s career she witnessed many individuals struggling with chronic pain and autoimmune symptoms that did not respond to the conventional medical treatment.

She saw the great need for understanding how the mind, body, brain and nervous system were intimately connected in healing these chronic conditions.

She enhanced her field of study and practice and became a specialist in the Healing Methods and Treatments for Mind Body and Neuroplastic symptoms and conditions, Stress Illness, Medically Unexplained Symptoms and other Psychophysiologial Disorders.

Dr Tovah studies and mentors with the doctors who further developed the TMS and Sarno methods of healing in the USA and Israel. 

​In 2019, Dr Tovah created a weekly broadcast in English and Hebrew and now hosts weekly.

“TMS RoundTable Recovery Global and TMS RoundTable Recovery Israel.

​This is a live interactive discussion sharing education, insights and tools for self healing chronic pain and autoimmune symptoms. Featured each week are health professionals, doctors, therapists, authors and movie directors all involved in this science of healing as well as many individuals that have self healed, in recovery and sharing their healing stories. You can view on YouTube.. 400 + recordings with various playlists specific information, resources and education. TMS RoundTable Recovery  

​Dr Tovah is originally from Philadelphia and now lives and works full time in Israel . She works in her local Chiropractic practice and international coaching progam.  She works with Teletherapy successfully assisting individuals all over the world. 

​She guides them in their personal and individual healing treatment plan and has successfully assisted many in their recovery from Chronic Pain and Autoimmune Symptoms

column22:
I am a therapist who primarily works with clients who have chronic pain. I am trained and experienced in assessing and treating chronic pain that has no structural cause, typically called neuroplastic pain, Psychophysiologic Disorders or Tension Myoneural Syndrome. I specifically utilize Pain Reprocessing Therapy to support my clients in eliminating or greatly reducing their pain. My therapeutic approach involves helping clients to stop fearing their pain and then processing their emotions through a lens of safety. I have also been trained in Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT), Mindfulness, Accelerated Resolution Therapy, Dialectical Behavioral Therapy (DBT), and Solution-Focused Therapy.

In my early 20s I began to experience chronic back pain, hip pain, shoulder pain, and headaches. I was diagnosed with several conditions at the time including fibromyalgia, and I felt deeply confused because doctors and healthcare providers could not agree upon what was physically wrong with me. After several years of suffering in daily pain and having little success with various treatments, I realized through my own research that nothing was structurally wrong with my body and in fact I had neuroplastic pain. Utilizing the same techniques I use with my clients, I recovered and have been pain free and extremely active ever since.

PPDA Board Member / ATNS Board Member

Rebecca Kennedy MD has been a family medicine physician for over 20 years with a lifelong interest in the mind-body connection and the body’s ability to heal itself. She worked at Kaiser Permanente in Portland, Oregon for 15 years and developed an intense curiosity to find better ways to help patients the current medical model fails. After years of searching, she found the groundbreaking work of Drs. Howard Schubiner and David Clarke and undertook extensive training in the model. She worked with hundreds of patients in the integrative clinic at Kaiser Permanente using this mind-body approach over several years. She ran the long COVID specialty group for the entire Northwest Kaiser Permanente region where she assessed hundreds of patients with long COVID, restoring many of them to good health with this mind-body approach. Witnessing the transformative impact of this model, Dr. Kennedy left Kaiser in 2023 to establish her own clinic and focus exclusively on mind-body medicine, called Resilience Health Care in Portland, Oregon. She provides virtual consultations for patients worldwide.  She is committed to spreading the word about this new science that can transform people’s lives.

Hi, my name is Elizabeth Jensen but I go by “Betsy”.

I am from Utah and I’m a single mom of four kids. I love hiking, skiing, and swing dancing. I have worked over 2 decades with people in pain as a Physical Therapist. But my own health struggles brought me to the mind-body approach. Now through coaching and my podcast, I help people understand pain science, how to rewire their brain, and how to calm their nervous system. This has helped people reduce or eliminate their chronic pain, instead of just managing it. I just renewed my Physical Therapist license and was happy to find so many continuing education courses on chronic pain, and treating “central sensitization” through this approach.

Telehealth available within Texas, Florida, and Colorado. As PRT coach, available everywhere else

 

As an Integrative Therapist, I’m ready to collaborate with you and empower you to build a positive mind-body-heart-and-soul connection for stress recovery and soaring above chronic pain, trauma, and anxiety. Mindfulness, nature, and creativity are three pillars of wellness in my counseling and coaching practice. Every client receives a unique blend of these three ingredients based on their needs and goals. For example, mindfulness skills play an integral role in Pain Reprocessing Therapy. As a certified practitioner of PRT, I teach tools to disrupt the pain-fear cycle and retrain the brain to feel safe using somatic tracking. I also incorporate Accelerated Resolution Therapy, an innovative eye movement and memory reconsolidation therapy, to neutralize triggers and rehearse success in the virtual reality of the mind. As an Ecotherapist and Mindful Outdoor Guide, I help people reconnect the body with physical activity and get active again using nature as a secure base. I also believe expressive arts have an important role to play in resolving painful emotions and facilitating the healing process. I invite an array of expressive arts into the therapeutic adventure, such as journaling, collage, poetry, watercolor art. My goal is to help you better get to know your inner wisdom and mobilize your inner resources for healing. You are the hero of your story, and I look forward to supporting you on your hero’s journey tohealing.

column22:
Do you have Chronic Pain? Migraine? IBS? Tinnitus? Or any of the other stressful and painful symptoms Of PPD?

If so, I truly empathise.

After many years of experiencing my own symptoms (resulting in a diagnosis of Fibromyalgia), I became interested in the mind-body connection on a Mindfulness retreat. Months of research, trial and error followed, until I eventually started to see my own results.

When I finally discovered how to overcome the debilitating symptoms of PPD/Stress Illness/TMS, I wanted to share this with my patients but couldn't in the NHS, so I set up my private practice in 2018 to support others to reach recovery.

As an accredited Cognitive Behavioural Psychotherapist and Mindfulness practitioner, I offer a support group, courses, workshops, and individual therapy.

Tecie has a unique educational background in both biochemistry and clinical social work. She specializes in treating chronic pain, functional neurological symptoms, dysautonomia, sleep disorders, and other neuroplastic symptoms. She has learned to explore how chronic stress and trauma influence health and physical sensations. She views each person through a lens of how their relationships and experiences have shaped their nervous system.


Tecie’s approach in psychotherapy is client-centered and always based on current research. She is passionate about utilizing her skills to support others in feeling grounded and well in their bodies. She also collaborates with medical teams and is a strong advocate for shifting the mindset and language used with stress-related symptoms within the medical community.


Tecie is licensed in both South Dakota and North Dakota. She sees clients in-person in Rapid City and offers telehealth in the surrounding areas.

column22:
Dr. Heather Martarella, PsyD, is a dedicated and compassionate pain psychologist. She began her journey of working with chronic pain patients during her postdoctoral fellowship at Kaiser in 2006, while also providing general clinical psychotherapy both prior to and after this period until 2014. An injury in 2012, which resulted in her own experience with chronic pain, inspired a career shift. By early 2014, after receiving additional education and professional experience, she began focusing exclusively on chronic pain management.

Dr. Martarella has held multiple roles in pain management, including serving as the Director of Compass Center for Functional Restoration at Sacramento Pain Clinic. In this role, she managed a multidisciplinary team, developed curricula, and provided pain neuroscience classes along with individual and group pain psychotherapy. She also worked as a Pain Psychologist at multiple Kaiser Pain Management and Pain Medicine Departments in South Sacramento, San Jose, and Napa/Vallejo.

She holds master’s and doctoral degrees in Clinical Psychology with neuropsychology and multicultural competency emphases from John F. Kennedy University. She completed postdoctoral fellowships at the University of San Francisco and Kaiser. Her academic contributions include roles as Department Chair, Director of Training, and Associate Professor at Argosy University, San Francisco Bay Area campus, along with teaching positions at various other universities.

Dr. Martarella is a past President of the Association of Family Therapists of Northern California and currently serves as the Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion Committee Chair for the American Association of Pain Psychology. She is known for her empathetic and educational approach and her dedication to providing hope as she helps those experiencing chronic pain find relief.

Yaicha is a board-certified Physician Associate (PA-C) with over 15 years of experience in healthcare and more than a decade of clinical practice. She holds a Bachelor's degree in Psychology and a Master’s in Physician Assistant Studies. As a psychiatric PA-C, Yaicha provides compassionate, evidence-based care for a range of mental health conditions, with a special focus on chronic pain and anxiety.

Yaicha is certified in Pain Reprocessing Therapy™, trained in the diagnosis and treatment of Neural Circuit Disorders, and specializes in the treatment of Chronic Pelvic Pain. In addition to her clinical training, she is a trauma-informed yoga teacher (RYT-240), Reiki Master, certified wellness coach, and breathwork and meditation teacher.

Yaicha’s work is grounded in the belief that healing is possible when the mind and body are treated as interconnected—and she is passionate about supporting others on their path to recovery.

Dr. Zafirides is a partner, co-founder and President of Central Ohio Behavioral Medicine. He is a graduate of The Ohio State University (OSU) College of Medicine.Dr. Zafirides completed his psychiatry residency at OSU Hospitals Department of Psychiatry. During his residency, he received awards in both clinical and teaching excellence. Dr. Zafirides also served as chief resident in his final year. Dr. Zafirides and his partners formed Central Ohio Behavioral Medicine in 1997.

As a board-certified psychiatrist, Dr. Zafirides has extensive experience in the evaluation and medical management of depression, anxiety disorders, ADHD, bipolar disorder and schizophrenia. His specific area of interest within psychiatry is the mind-body role of emotions and physical illness, especially chronic pain. Dr. Zafirides has been published in this area of psychiatry, lecturing both nationally and internationally on this topic. With an existential orientation to psychotherapy, Dr. Zafirides has been greatly influenced by the work of Irvin Yalom, Viktor Frankl and Rollo May.

Dr. Zafirides is a member of the American Psychiatric Association and the Ohio Psychiatric Physicians Association. He is member of the Psychophysiologic Disorders Association (PPDA). He serves as an advisory board member for the National Institute of Whole Health. He is also a guest lecturer of psychopharmacology to graduate students in the OSU Counselor Education Program.

Dr. Zafirides has received the Best Doctors In America recognition multiple times since 2005. He has also been recognized nationally with multiple Patient’s Choice Awards and Compassionate Doctor Awards since 2008.

column22: Terri Nishimoto PT, CLT, PRPC has been a physical therapist that has specialized in pelvic health physical therapy for over 25 years. She works with men and women with pelvic pain, or pelvic floor dysfunction. She treats all of her patients with a holistic approach that encompasses many different modalities. She has certifications in lymphatic drainage, Feldenkrais method, and pelvic rehabilitation. In addition to treating patients she teaches the pelvic health section for the University of Colorado Physical Therapy Program.
